original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded protocolFeePercent can make sells revert (funds effectively trapped) due to fee underflow on sell payout
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Cred.setProtocolFeePercent() has no upper bound. If protocolFeePercent is set high enough such that protocolFee + creatorFee >= price on sells, then `_handleTrade` and batch sell paths compute `price - protocolFee - creatorFee` and will revert in Solidity 0.8+, blocking sells for affected creds/curators. This can lock curator positions economically (cannot exit) and potentially freeze trading liquidity.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete code defect (missing bounds) with clear failure mode: arithmetic underflow revert during normal sell flow. It violates the expected economic invariant that sells should always have a non-negative payout and should not brick exits under reasonable configuration.
deployment_assumptions: Owner (or compromised owner key) sets protocolFeePercent to a high value (e.g., > 10000 or high enough combined with royalties to exceed price) after users have acquired shares
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Cred.initialize sets protocolFeePercent=500 in Deploy.s.sol, but there is no on-chain bound in Cred.setProtocolFeePercent; future changes are unconstrained.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No cap on protocolFeePercent; sell payout implicitly uses `price - protocolFees[i] - creatorFees[i]` (batch totalPayout uses same subtraction), which will revert on underflow

preconditions: ProtocolFeePercent is set high enough (or combined with sellShareRoyalty) that protocolFee + creatorFee >= price on sells for some supply/amount
Users hold shares and attempt to sell via batchSellShareCred

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Sell paths can become non-executable (revert) when fees exceed or equal price
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No `require(protocolFeePercent_ <= 10_000)` and no explicit check `price > protocolFee + creatorFee` for sells

analysis_questions: What maximum protocolFeePercent (and royalty combos) cause sell underflow for realistic supply/amounts?
Does any other path (single sell or batch) explicitly check fee sum vs price?
Can users still exit through alternative mechanisms (none visible)?
validation_requirements: Property test over fee percent/royalty rates and sell amounts to detect revert thresholds
Confirm batchSellShareCred always reaches subtraction path before any other revert that masks it
Simulate owner raising fees post-deposits and verify sell bricking

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After users buy shares, owner sets protocolFeePercent extremely high. Subsequent batchSellShareCred calls revert because payout becomes negative, effectively trapping users in positions (cannot exit).

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Users can be prevented from selling shares; capital locked and price discovery broken
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Any share holders attempting to sell
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires owner to lower fee or upgrade contract; otherwise positions remain stuck

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests asserting fee bounds or sell liveness under fee/royalty extremes

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor fee changes and sell revert reasons on-chain

validation_result.mechanism: unbounded fee parameter causing arithmetic underflow on sell
validation_result.impact_summary: If owner sets protocolFeePercent high enough (alone or combined with sellShareRoyalty), all sell operations revert due to Solidity 0.8 underflow, permanently trapping user share positions.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The Cred.setProtocolFeePercent function has no upper bound check, unlike PhiFactory.setProtocolFee which caps at 10,000. The sell payout is computed as `price - protocolFee - creatorFee` in both _handleTrade and _validateAndCalculateBatch. When protocolFeePercent + sellShareRoyalty >= 10,000 (i.e., combined fees >= 100% of price), this subtraction underflows. Since Solidity 0.8.25 has built-in overflow/underflow checks, this causes an automatic revert, making all sell paths non-executable. The underflow occurs in multiple locations: the priceLimit check (`price - protocolFee - creatorFee < priceLimit`), the payout transfer (`curator_.safeTransferETH(price - protocolFee - creatorFee)`), and the batch totalAmount calculation (`totalAmount += prices[i] - protocolFees[i] - creatorFees[i]`). Users who have purchased shares before the fee change cannot exit their positions.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms: (1) setProtocolFeePercent has no bounds check at Cred.sol lines ~118-122, (2) sell payout subtraction at Cred.sol _handleTrade uses unchecked `price - protocolFee - creatorFee`, (3) batch sell path has identical subtraction in _validateAndCalculateBatch. Compare with PhiFactory.setProtocolFee which explicitly checks `if (protocolFee_ > 10_000) revert ProtocolFeeTooHigh()` — proving the developers intended to have such a bound but missed it in Cred.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No upper bound validation on protocolFeePercent_. Any value including >10000 (>100%) is accepted. Compare with PhiFactory.setProtocolFee which has `if (protocolFee_ > 10_000) revert ProtocolFeeTooHigh()`.

Both the priceLimit check and the payout transfer compute `price - protocolFee - creatorFee`. If protocolFee + creatorFee > price (due to unbounded protocolFeePercent), this underflows and reverts in Solidity 0.8.25.
Same underflow issue in the batch sell validation path, affecting both the priceLimit check and the totalAmount accumulation.

calling_sequence: 1. Owner calls setProtocolFeePercent(10001) — no validation, accepted
5. In sell branch: price - protocolFee - creatorFee underflows → automatic revert
6. All sell attempts fail, shares are trapped
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: sellShareCred → _handleTrade → BondingCurve.getPriceData returns protocolFee > price → _handleTrade sell branch: `price - protocolFee - creatorFee` underflows → revert. The same path applies for batchSellShareCred → _validateAndCalculateBatch → `prices[i] - protocolFees[i] - creatorFees[i]` underflows → revert.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Owner (or compromised owner key) sets protocolFeePercent to a high value (e.g., > 10000 or high enough combined with royalties to exceed price) after users have acquired shares
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deploy.s.sol initializes Cred with protocolFeePercent_=500 (5%), which is safe. However, setProtocolFeePercent has no on-chain bound, so any future owner call can set it arbitrarily high. PhiFactory.setProtocolFee includes `if (protocolFee_ > 10_000) revert ProtocolFeeTooHigh()` showing the developers intended such bounds but omitted them in Cred.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) Users buy shares in various creds, locking ETH in the bonding curve. (2) Owner (or attacker who compromised owner key) calls setProtocolFeePercent(10001). (3) Any user attempting sellShareCred or batchSellShareCred will have their transaction revert due to underflow in `price - protocolFee - creatorFee`. (4) Even with protocolFeePercent=5001 and a cred with sellShareRoyalty=5000, the combined 100.01% fee causes the same underflow. (5) Users cannot exit their positions. Recovery requires owner to lower the fee, but a malicious or compromised owner may not do so.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. The missing bounds check is a clear code defect. If exploited (by malicious owner or compromised key), all sell operations across all creds become impossible, trapping user capital in bonding curve positions with no alternative exit path. The defect is confirmed regardless of exploitation feasibility because the code lacks input validation that was clearly intended (as evidenced by PhiFactory having the check).
recommended_mitigations: Add bounds check to setProtocolFeePercent: `require(protocolFeePercent_ <= 10_000, 'Fee too high');`
Add a combined fee safety check ensuring protocolFeePercent + MAX_ROYALTY_RANGE < RATIO_BASE (e.g., require protocolFeePercent <= 5000)
Add an explicit check in sell paths: `require(price > protocolFee + creatorFee, 'Fees exceed price')` with graceful handling
Consider a timelock on fee parameter changes to give users time to exit positions before fee increases take effect
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Cred and BondingCurve contracts with standard config. 2. Create a cred and have a user buy shares. 3. As owner, call setProtocolFeePercent(10001). 4. Attempt sellShareCred — verify it reverts with arithmetic underflow. 5. Also test with protocolFeePercent=5001 and sellShareRoyalty=5000 to show combined fee overflow.
uncertainties: The exact threshold depends on the specific sellShareRoyalty per cred — different creds have different thresholds
Whether the owner multisig/governance has off-chain safeguards that prevent setting unreasonable values (but on-chain there is no protection)
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test setProtocolFeePercent with various values and verify sell behavior
Test edge case where protocolFeePercent + sellShareRoyalty == 10000 exactly (zero payout, may not revert but payout is 0)
Verify that buy path also handles extreme protocolFeePercent gracefully (buy still requires msg.value >= price + fees, so high fees just make buying more expensive rather than reverting)
